nameMeanings.name: Logan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from Logan (Ayrshire) or from some other minor place of this name.
Irish: from Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’, a personal name (earlier spelled Lothchán) of unexplained etymology. See also Duck .

History: James Logan (1674–1751), an Ulster Scot and a Quaker, born in Lurgan, County Armagh, Ireland, became secretary to William Penn and held high office in the state of PA. Among his descendants have been numbered several prominent statesmen. KY pioneer Benjamin Logan moved with his parents from West Pennsborough Township, Cumberland County, PA, to Augusta County, VA, in 1740.

variants.name: Lohan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.  German Gerhard, Hannelore.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan was born in 1460, in Hilton, Berwickshire, Scotland. She had at least 1 daughter. She died in 1521, in her hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan est née en 1460, à Hilton, Berwickshire, Écosse. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1521, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan wurde 1460 in Hilton, Berwickshire, Scotland geboren. Sie hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1521 im Alter von 61 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan è nata il nel 1460 nel Hilton, Berwickshire, Scotland. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1521, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.Whitsome and Hilton, Berwickshire, Scotl: スコットランドバーウィクシュアイアヒルトン

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan(은)는 1460년 스컷랜드 버윅실르 힐턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1521년 그녀의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan nasceu em 1460, em Hilton, Berwickshire, Scotland. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1521, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan родилась в 1460 г. в местности хилтон, бирвиккшир, Шотландия. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1521 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Logan nació en 1460, en Hilton, Berwickshire, Scotland. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1521, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
